name: manage_knowledge
summary: Read or write pages in the shared knowledge base — durable facts injected into every ticket's system prompt.

When pages exist, their one-line summaries appear under `## Knowledge` in your system prompt. If that section is absent, the store is empty. Use `read` to load a full page body, or `list` to see all pages.
constraints: Save when work reveals a durable fact future tickets need.
Each page covers one topic. Slug is descriptive or file-path-derived: `deployment-config`, `pkg-utils-py`.
The summary is one sentence shown in the index. Keep it under 80 chars.
Use `[[slug]]` wikilinks in page content to cross-reference related pages.
`write` is an upsert: it overwrites the full page. Use `read` first if appending.
The index has a char limit. When full, consolidate: merge pages, remove stale ones.
IMPORTANT: `write` requires slug, summary, and content — omitting any one returns an error.
IMPORTANT: only call `read` for a slug you have already seen in `## Knowledge` or via `list`. If you have not seen the slug, the page does not exist.
Example write: {"action":"write","slug":"deployment-config","summary":"API server listens on port 3000","content":"# Deployment Config\n\nPort 3000. Rate limit: 100 req/min."}
anti_patterns: Saving task progress or TODOs. Those belong in tickets.
Creating a new page for every small fact. Consolidate related facts.
Calling `write` without slug — the most common error; all three fields are required.
Calling `read` for a slug not confirmed via `## Knowledge` or `list` — the page does not exist.

action.description: The operation to perform. 'write' upserts a page (requires slug + summary + content). 'read' returns the full page body (requires slug; only valid for slugs in the index). 'remove' deletes a page (requires slug). 'list' returns the current index.

slug.description: Page identifier (lowercase, hyphens, max 60 chars). Required for write, read, and remove — omitting it returns an error. For file paths, replace dots and slashes with hyphens (e.g. pkg/utils.py → pkg-utils-py).

summary.description: One-line index entry shown in the knowledge index. Required for write. Keep under 80 chars.

content.description: Full page body in markdown. Required for write. May include [[wikilinks]].
